title: Peter Magyar wins two-thirds parliamentary majority in earthquake Hungary election
subhead: Incumbent Viktor Orban, who has led the country for 16 years, has conceded the election and called the results 'painful'.

subhead: Representatives of the two countries will meet in Washington, DC on Tuesday.

title: In war-torn Lebanon, artists and cultural actors mobilize to support displaced families
subhead: Several spontaneous cultural initiatives, including in theaters, aim to help people driven from their homes by Israeli bombardments.

title: 'They are asked to be both Madonna and whore': Photographer Philippa James captures her teenage daughter's daily life on social media
subhead: In her series 'Once a Slag…,' the British photographer explores the contradictions facing young people as they navigate identity, social pressures and the constant exposure of their lives online.

title: 'Do you have any regrets about having to kill?' On Trans-Siberian Railway, Russian soldiers recount time in Ukraine
subhead: 'On Board the Trans-Siberian' (2/2). As the train speeds toward the Russian Far East, between Novosibirsk and Vladivostok, the brutality of the war in Ukraine is at the center of conversations between soldiers on leave and 'ordinary' passengers.

Thomas Piketty: '1789, 1945, 2026: Three French debt crises'
subhead: To tackle public debt, France remains wealthy with solid underlying economic fundamentals. Yet leaders must confront prevailing 'political conservatism' and the wealthiest's behavior, argues economist Thomas Piketty.

title: 'I stared death in the face!' On board the Trans-Siberian, a 9,000-kilometer journey through an exhausted Russia
subhead: 'On Board the Trans-Siberian' (1/2). In late December 2025 and early 2026, on the train linking Moscow to Vladivostok, soldiers returning from Ukraine cross paths with cautious travelers, careful about what they say. Beyond the Urals, propaganda posters attempt by every possible means to recruit new soldiers.

title: The planning battle to revive Tour Montparnasse, Paris's only skyscraper
subhead: After more than 10 years of negotiations between Paris City Hall and the tower's co-owners, the transformation of the now-empty Tour Montparnasse and its property complex is set to begin in autumn 2026.
byline: Audrey Lagadec,  Emeline Cazi and  Isabelle Regnier
